title: Chapter 40: Buying the Sky
content: The flying entities came the next day.
They didn't come through rifts. They simply appeared — coalescing from the violet sky itself. Shapes of shadow and bone, wings that beat without sound, eyes that burned like embers.
There were dozens of them. Circling. Waiting.
"They're assessing," Silas said. "Waiting for orders."
"Who's giving the orders?"
"The same entity you suspended the Obligation against. It's found a workaround."
Roxanne looked up at the circling shapes.
"Can I buy the air?"
"You can buy air rights. Territorial control extends vertically as well as horizontally in the Nether-Grid."
"Then buy them."
She held up the card.
The interface appeared.

*PURCHASE: AIR RIGHTS — VANCE DEMESNE AIRSPACE
COST: 500,000 INFINITY NOTES
DURATION: PERMANENT
CONFIRM?*
"Confirm."
The card flashed.
Above the campus, the violet sky rippled.

The flying entities stopped mid-circle. They hovered, confused. Then, one by one, they began to descend — not by choice, but because the air they were flying through no longer belonged to them. They were trespassing in purchased airspace.
They landed on the quad. Surrounded. Grounded.
The Executioner stepped forward, chainsaw crackling.
The entities did not resist. They had no orders for this scenario.
Frost approached the largest one.
"You are in violation of territorial air rights. By the terms of the original contract, you are subject to seizure."
The entity's burning eyes flickered.
"Seizure?"
"Confiscation. Your forms are now property of the Vance Demesne."
Roxanne walked up to the entity. It was twice her height. Made of shadow and hunger.
"You work for me now."
"We serve the 11th Layer."
"You served the 11th Layer. Now you serve me. The contract is clear."
The entity looked at its companions. They were all on the ground. All surrounded.
"...What would you have us do?"
Roxanne smiled.
"Fly patrol. Keep the sky safe. And if the 11th Layer sends more of you, tell them the air is taken."
